Table 8.8: Results using a larger spatial domain: zmin = ln(105)− 10, zmax = ln(105)+ 10 (baseline domain zmin = ln(105)− 8, zmax = ln(105) + 8, Table 8.5) for z ∈ {s, b}.
Table 8.11: Constant boundary conditions on a larger domain, compared with our asymptotic boundary conditions (3.17).
Table 8.10: Constant boundary conditions on the baseline domain, compared with our asymptotic boundary conditions (3.17).

Figure 8.1: 2D Kou jump–diffusion test case. Panels (a)–(b) show representative 1D slices of the target CF G and the fitted CF Ĝ, and panel (c) shows a representative slice of the trained density ĝ. To improve conditioning for oscillatory G, the CF targets in the loss function are affinely rescaled as in Remark 5.1 of [11]; the plotted slices are shown in the original (unrescaled) coordinates.

Table 8.3: Estimated annualized parameters for the 2D Kou model with idiosyncratic and common jumps, calibrated from CRSP equity index and 10-year Treasury total returns; monthly, 1926:01– 2024:12. Threshold technique from [9].
Figure 8.2: Pre-commitment mean–CVaR optimal control heat map (allocation weight in component s; interpreted as equity in the DC illustration).
Table 8.7: Convergence test, mean–CVaR with γ = 10 and α = 0.05. Parameters in Table 8.3. Brackets show half-widths of 99% confidence intervals. Units: thousands of currency units.
Figure 8.3: Efficient frontier of mean–CVaR with α = 0.05, computed on the finest refinement level (DC illustration).
